event: Portuguese navigators became the first Europeans to sight the Chagos Archipelago while exploring the Indian Ocean.
French settlers from Mauritius (Isle de France) began establishing coconut plantations and bringing enslaved Africans to the previously uninhabited Chagos islands.
Britain attempted an early, short-lived settlement on Diego Garcia before withdrawing.
The French firmly established a permanent coconut-oil plantation settlement on Diego Garcia, founding the community of the Chagossians (Ilois).
Britain captured Mauritius and its dependencies, including the Chagos Archipelago, from France during the Napoleonic Wars.
The Treaty of Paris formally ceded Mauritius and the Chagos Archipelago to Britain, which administered Chagos as a dependency of Mauritius.
Slavery was abolished in the British colony, transforming the Chagos plantation labour force into indentured and contract workers.
Coconut and copra plantations flourished across Diego Garcia, Peros Banhos and Salomon, sustaining a settled Chagossian Creole-speaking population.
The German cruiser SMS Emden visited Diego Garcia during the First World War, with islanders unaware that war had broken out.
The Chagos Agalega Company took over management of the plantations, the last major commercial operator on the islands.
Britain detached the Chagos Archipelago from Mauritius and islands from the Seychelles to create the British Indian Ocean Territory (BIOT).
Britain and the United States signed an agreement making BIOT available for the defence purposes of both governments for an initial 50 years.
The British Crown bought out the plantation companies, taking full ownership of the land in preparation for clearing the islands.
Chagossians who travelled abroad began to be refused return, marking the start of the forced depopulation of the territory.
Construction of the major US naval and military base on Diego Garcia began, and the remaining islanders' food supplies were cut off.
The last Chagossians were forcibly removed and deported to Mauritius and the Seychelles, completing the depopulation of the archipelago.
The islands of Aldabra, Farquhar and Desroches were returned to the Seychelles upon its independence, leaving BIOT comprising only the Chagos Archipelago.
Britain agreed a compensation settlement with Mauritius for resettled Chagossians, who signed renunciation forms many later contested.
The Diego Garcia base played a logistical role for US operations, becoming a strategic Indian Ocean staging point during the Cold War.
Diego Garcia served as a key launch and support base for US bombers during the Gulf War against Iraq.
The High Court of England and Wales ruled that the 1971 ordinance banning Chagossians from the islands was unlawful.
Diego Garcia became a major forward base for US air operations in Afghanistan following the September 11 attacks.
Britain granted full British citizenship to Chagossians and their descendants under the British Overseas Territories Act.
The British government used Orders in Council to reimpose the ban on resettlement, overriding the 2000 court ruling.
The House of Lords upheld the government's right to bar the Chagossians' return, reversing lower court victories.
Britain declared a Marine Protected Area around the Chagos Archipelago, one of the world's largest, drawing criticism that it blocked resettlement.
A Permanent Court of Arbitration tribunal ruled the Marine Protected Area was established in breach of international law regarding Mauritius's rights.
Britain extended the UK-US defence agreement for Diego Garcia and announced it would not allow Chagossian resettlement, citing cost and security.
The International Court of Justice issued an advisory opinion that the UK's separation of Chagos was unlawful, and the UN General Assembly demanded its return to Mauritius.
A UN tribunal ruling on maritime boundaries reaffirmed that Mauritius, not the UK, holds sovereignty over the Chagos Archipelago.
Britain and Mauritius opened formal negotiations over the future sovereignty of the British Indian Ocean Territory.
Britain agreed in principle to cede sovereignty of the Chagos Archipelago to Mauritius while retaining a long-term lease over the Diego Garcia base.
